steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the area scaling rule
steps.content: For similar figures, if the linear scale factor is $k$, then the area scale factor is $k^2$. Since each side of EFGH is $6$ times the corresponding side of ABCD, we have $k = 6$, so areas scale by $6^2 = 36$.

steps.title: Calculate the new area
steps.content: Multiply the area of ABCD by the area scale factor: $54 \times 36 = 1,944$ square units. That confirms D is correct.
